design_philosophy.core_loop: Combat platforming cycle: advance through level, defeat enemies with sword and energy bolts, reach boss, defeat boss to progress. Rocket pack provides aerial mobility and traversal options.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigating enemy-filled levels while managing limited rocket pack usage and positioning for optimal sword/bolt combat against escalating threats culminating in boss encounters.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Sparkster, a Rocket Knight opossum, using sword combat combined with jetpack flight to overcome an invading army, rescue a princess, and save the kingdom from destruction.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid platform-shooter identity ('platform blaster') that refuses pure platformer conventions; emphasis on combat projectiles and jet mobility over precise jumping. Difficulty structure is opinionated: different final boss and narrative payoff based on chosen difficulty, with hard mode extending the story beyond normal completion.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty modes (Easy, Normal, Hard) that fundamentally alter progression structure. Easy mode ends with Axel Gear boss and shorter level count. Normal mode continues to Generalissimo Lioness. Hard mode extends further with additional true final stage. This creates meaningful replay incentives and allows players to self-select challenge level. Critics noted 'huge challenge' in reviews.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty-based gating: Hard mode unlocks extended narrative and additional final stage unavailable on lower difficulties. Secret hard modes mentioned as unlockable content.
replayability_hooks: Three distinct difficulty modes with different boss sequences and story conclusions
Secret hard modes unlock additional challenge content
Multiple stage variations (Easy mode uses different level count than Normal/Hard)
Specialized level types (auto-scrolling ostrich riding stage, top-down shooter stage) provide mechanical variety

world_building_techniques: Kingdom of Eginasem setting (opossums vs. invading Lioness army)
Character archetypes: Princess Flora (kidnapped), Axel Gear (rival Rocket Knight antagonist)
Military invasion narrative framing with escalating stakes (warhead threatening planet destruction)
Visual character design establishes fantasy world (opossum knight protagonist, yellow wolf/dog soldiers, robot enemies)

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating antagonist hierarchy: encounters progress from Axel Gear (rival Rocket Knight, mid-game pivot point) to Generalissimo Lioness (true final threat). Boss design emphasizes pattern recognition and combat positioning. Described as 'gigantic bosses' by Electronic Gaming Monthly.
notable_bosses: Axel Gear (Rival Rocket Knight - serves as final boss on Easy mode, mid-game boss on Normal/Hard)
Generalissimo Lioness (Leader of invading wolf army, true final boss on Normal mode)
Unnamed additional boss tier (exists in Hard mode extension beyond standard progression)

faction_indicators: Lioness's Invading Army (yellow dog/wolf soldiers - antagonist faction)
Rocket Knights (elite combat order; both Sparkster and Axel Gear belong, indicating ideological or military schism)
danger_signals: Princess Flora's kidnapping establishes immediate threat to social order
Axel Gear's betrayal (aiding invading forces) indicates internal threat alongside external invasion
Warhead deployment plan signals existential danger to entire planet, escalating stakes beyond kingdom level
environmental_storytelling: Robot and mechanical enemies establish technological military invasion force
Progression through enemy territory (increasing density/difficulty) suggests advancing occupation
Final infiltration of enemy battleship represents penetration to command authority
